summary: Batch match-progress PR that reported 14 matched functions and 8 failed attempts across stages, items, game/menu code, and status/HUD code. The final merged diff was small and focused: it rewrote mn_80230198 in mnmainrule.c into a switch-based mode dispatch, expanded grHomeRun_GroundVars layout with padding and bitfields, adjusted a gm_1601.h prototype from s32 to int, and added an explicit default break in itlugia.c. The author later noted the work was split into subsystem draft PRs for easier review and that several commits were dropped because equivalent or better decompilations were already upstream.

key_files.role: Menu rules animation matching cleanup for mn_80230198
Stage GroundVars layout expansion for Home-Run Contest related work

what_changed: PR body reported 14 matched functions: grOldKongo_80210650, it_802EB6DC, fn_8021E994, mn_80230198, gm_80164910, it_802D1830, ifStatus_802F7034, itMatadogas_UnkMotion1_Anim, fn_80162068, it_802E2470, itOldottosea_UnkMotion7_Phys, mn_80230D18, it_2725_Logic8_DmgReceived, and itZrshell_UnkMotion10_Anim.
PR body also recorded 8 failed attempts, including grPura_80212FC0 at best 10.3% and fn_8024FD40 at best 27.9%, with several others listed as unknown percentage.

mnmainrule.c changed mn_80230198 from a nested conditional/goto-shaped implementation using arg2/option into a switch on a renamed mode parameter. Active cases 0, 2, and 4 perform the frame lookup; cases 1, 3, 5, and 6 break without work.

mnmainrule.c kept the frame-range table strategy: read the current frame with mn_8022F298, scan the first five entries of mn_803EC770 and mn_803EC734, then call mn_8022ED6C with the selected AnimLoopSettings pointer.
mnmainrule.c normalized float literals in mn_803EC734 and mn_803EC770 from lowercase f suffixes to uppercase F suffixes.
gr/types.h expanded grHomeRun_GroundVars with explicit gp-relative offset comments, pad_D4[0x14], and a union exposing xE8 both as a raw byte and as bitfields xE8_b0 through xE8_b7.
gm_1601.h changed fn_80161C90's second parameter type from s32 to int.
itlugia.c added an explicit default: break arm to the switch in it_802D24A0 before applying the selected multiplier to ip->x40_vel.z.
Issue comment said the large PR was split into four smaller draft PRs by subsystem: #2402 ft fighters, #2404 gr stages, #2405 gm+mn game loop/menu, and #2406 it items.
Issue comment said commits for lbAudioAx_800243F4/fn_800244F4, grOldPupupu_8021119C, and fn_8023DAEC in mnnamenew.c were dropped because upstream already had equivalent or better decompilations.
smart_moves: Converted mn_80230198's sparse mode checks into a clear switch while preserving the matching behavior shown in the diff; this makes the accepted modes 0, 2, and 4 explicit and avoids carrying misleading goto labels forward.
Renamed the mn_80230198 third parameter from arg2 to mode, a small semantic naming improvement supported by the new switch structure.
Modeled grHomeRun_GroundVars with both raw-byte and bitfield access for xE8, which is a reusable pattern when a field must match layout but future code may need individual flag bits.
Inserted explicit padding in grHomeRun_GroundVars rather than leaving the gap implicit, making the gp+C4 through gp+E8 layout searchable and less error-prone.
Added a default break in the Lugia item switch, likely preserving intentional no-op behavior for unhandled cases without changing the later multiplier application.

Split a broad cross-subsystem match-progress branch into smaller subsystem PRs for easier review, and dropped commits already superseded upstream instead of preserving duplicate or worse decompilations.
decomp_lessons: For menu animation code, a clean switch over sparse mode values can still match and is preferable to preserving an artificial nested-if/goto reconstruction when the control-flow shape allows it.

Frame-range lookup code can be matched by keeping the table order and pointer assignment pattern intact: check one animation table, then the alternate table, and pass the selected settings pointer to the animation helper.
Unknown stage data should be locked down with explicit padding, offset comments, and raw/bitfield unions when a match depends on exact GroundVars layout.
Plain int versus project typedefs such as s32 may matter for source-shape consistency in headers even when the ABI size is equivalent; align prototypes with the matched implementation.

Large generated or WIP match batches are vulnerable to upstream drift. Before finalizing, compare against upstream and remove equivalent or better decompilations rather than resolving conflicts around obsolete work.

When a PR body records many matches but the final diff is much smaller, future archaeology should inspect split follow-up PRs and merge commit text before assuming every listed match landed in this PR's final file diff.
naming_conventions: Keep address-based names for unresolved functions, e.g. mn_80230198, gm_80164910, fn_80162068, it_802D1830, and grOldKongo_80210650.
Use semantic parameter names when evidence is available from control flow; this PR changed arg2 to mode in mn_80230198.
Unknown struct members follow x-offset names such as xC4, xC6, xC8, xCC, xD0, and xE8.
Padding fields use pad_OFFSET style, shown here as pad_D4.
Bitfields derived from an unknown byte use xOFFSET_bN names, shown here as xE8_b0 through xE8_b7.
Item motion function names combine item identity and motion role when known, e.g. itMatadogas_UnkMotion1_Anim, itOldottosea_UnkMotion7_Phys, and itZrshell_UnkMotion10_Anim.
Project casing is preserved across subsystem prefixes, e.g. ifStatus_802F7034, ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0, and gmClassic_801B3A34.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use switch-case grouping to express sparse accepted values while matching the original branch behavior.
Keep PAD_STACK(8) in mn_80230198, indicating stack layout still mattered for the match.
Use an i < 5 loop bound for the animation table scan in mn_80230198 rather than the previous i != 5 spelling.
Assign the candidate AnimLoopSettings pointer before each frame-range test so the selected pointer is already available when the loop breaks.
Add explicit default: break cases in switches when unhandled values should produce no extra action but the source shape needs a default arm.
Represent gp-relative stage memory with explicit pad arrays and bitfield unions to make loads/stores around unknown flags match without inventing semantic field names.
review_feedback: No review comments and no formal reviews were present in the slice.
decomp-dev bot reported for GALE01: No changes.
Author process comment said to split the PR into four smaller subsystem draft PRs for easier review: #2402 ft, #2404 gr, #2405 gm+mn, and #2406 it.
Author process comment said several commits were dropped because upstream already had equivalent or better decompilations.
Evidence note: the author comment says 'Closing this PR in favor of the split ones,' but the PR metadata in the slice says the PR was merged on 2026-05-10.
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #2402 for the ft/ fighter matches split out of this batch.
Inspect PR #2404 for the gr/ stage matches split out of this batch, especially grHomeRun_GroundVars and fn_8021E994.
Inspect PR #2405 for the gm/ + mn/ game-loop and menu matches, including mn_80230198 and mn_80230D18.
Inspect PR #2406 for the it/ item matches, including itlugia, itoldottosea, itmatadogas, ittincle, and itzgshell functions.
Search upstream history for lbAudioAx_800243F4, fn_800244F4, grOldPupupu_8021119C, and fn_8023DAEC to understand which better decompilations replaced dropped commits.
Check later uses of grHomeRun_GroundVars.xE8_b* to see whether the bitfield names gained semantic names.
